title: Sports and leisure assistants
description: All artists and related workers not listed separately. Compete in athletic events. Play prerecorded music for live audiences at venues or events such as clubs, parties, or wedding receptions. May use techniques such as mixing, cutting, or sampling to manipulate recordings. May also perform as emcee (master of ceremonies). (Based on 38 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prepare collection reports for submission to supervisors.
Teach skills, such as proper climbing methods, and demonstrate and advise on the use of equipment.
Provide for physical safety of groups, performing activities like first aid or directing evacuations.
Greet and register visitors, issuing required identification badges or safety devices.
Perform minor repairs or adjustments to slot machines to resolve issues.
Conduct educational activities for school children and lead guided tours while describing points of interest.
Monitor visitors' activities for compliance with regulations and safety practices during tours or events.
Provide directions and other pertinent information to visitors regarding tour sites and operations.
Participate in athletic events or competitive sports, following established rules and regulations.
Exercise or practice under the direction of athletic trainers or coaches to develop skills and prepare for competitions.
Ensure safety rules are followed and report or remove safety hazards and disruptive guests.
Supervise staff, games, and mediate disputes to ensure efficient operations and compliance with regulations.
Determine cash requirements for windows and order necessary currency, coins, or chips for gaming operations.
Lead teams by serving as captain and assess performance following athletic competitions.
Supervise and coordinate the work activities of personnel, including training staff members and assigning work duties.
Organize, lead, and promote interest in recreational activities, such as sports, games, arts, and hobbies.
Evaluate recreation areas, facilities, and services to determine if they are producing desired results.
Assess the needs and interests of individuals and groups and plan activities accordingly, given the available equipment or facilities.
Provide emergency first aid and summon medical assistance when necessary.
Greet new arrivals to activities, introducing them to other participants and encouraging participation.
Enforce rules and regulations of recreational facilities to maintain discipline and ensure safety.
Document individuals' progress toward meeting treatment goals and provide requested information on students' progress.
Develop program plans for individuals or assist in plan development and communicate with staff to resolve problems with individual students.
Meet and collaborate with agency personnel, community organizations, and other professionals to plan balanced recreational programs for participants.
Promote or provide wildlife safety training and address groups to disseminate information about conservation and regulations.
Inspect commercial operations relating to wildlife, recreation, or protected areas, and assess conditions or damage due to wildlife or human activity.
Investigate wildlife-related complaints, damages, and advise on preventive measures or wildlife management revisions.
Provide assistance and information to visitors at parks or recreational areas, including safety and wildlife interaction.
Patrol recreational areas and monitor activities to ensure compliance with safety regulations and address hazardous conditions.
Instruct participants in recreational activities such as skiing or swimming and provide safety precaution information.
Perform traffic control duties in recreational settings, and guide pedestrians or vehicles to ensure safety.
Document and maintain records of incidents, conditions, and observations in recreational or wildlife areas.
Act as part of the security team at transportation terminals, hotels, or similar establishments.
Inspect guests' rooms to ensure they are adequately stocked, orderly, and comfortable.
Investigate disturbances on the premises, such as security alarms, altercations, and suspicious activity.
Inspect and adjust security equipment to ensure it is operational or to detect evidence of tampering.
Explain company policies and procedures to staff using oral or written communication.
Transport guests about premises and local areas, or arrange for transportation.
Call police or fire departments in emergencies, such as fire, bomb threats, and presence of unauthorized persons.
Apprehend or evict trespassers, rule violators, or other security threats from the premises.
Advise employees in handling problems or resolving complaints from customers, tenants, or other persons.
Assign security personnel to posts or patrols and ensure they follow established procedures.
Deliver and pick up items, such as newspapers, documents, and packages, between departments and establishments.
Plan and follow the most efficient routes for delivering goods and confirm deliveries with the home office.
Inspect work areas and equipment to ensure conformance to cleanliness and maintenance standards.
Train workers in proper operational procedures and company policies and ensure compliance with specifications.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.
Recruit and hire staff members and assign work schedules to meet operational needs.
Meet with managers or supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations and workers.
